question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to ingest a fraudulent stream file containing manipulated smart contract state by injecting a crafted StreamFileSignature whose node reports an artificially low totalStake as the first element in the collection, thereby lowering the stakeRequiredForConsensus threshold so that a minority of colluding nodes can satisfy canReachConsensus(), resulting in unintended smart contract behavior reflected by the mirror node?
